title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard for enabling AI applications to connect with external tools, APIs, and services through a common interface. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports both local and remote server configurations, becoming essential for building AI agents across frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard that lets AI applications connect to external tools, APIs, and services through a common interface, and breaks down its architecture into client, server, and the communication layer between them. The protocol relies on JSON-RPC for client-server messaging and supports both local and remote server configurations. The relevance is its role in building AI agents across frameworks including LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop, where a shared interface replaces ad hoc integrations.
